title: DTSN change not triggering Trickle reset for downward route consistency
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/dodag.c:270-352

RFC 6550 Section 6.3.1 specifies that DTSN (DAO Trigger Sequence Number) is used to maintain downward route consistency. When a node detects a DTSN change, it should trigger DAO updates.

The current implementation parses DTSN from DIOs:
```c
dio->dtsn = data[5];
```

But `lichen_rpl_dodag_process_dio` does not track or compare DTSN values, nor does it signal the need for DAO retransmission when DTSN changes.

Impact: Changes to the downward routing topology may not propagate correctly, leading to stale routes at the DODAG root.

Suggested fix: 
1. Store current DTSN in the dodag struct
2. When DTSN changes, return a flag or call a callback to trigger DAO transmission
3. Consider integrating with Trickle timer for DAO scheduling
close_reason: Added dtsn to dodag struct; updated init, adopt_version, process_dio to track DTSN and return 1 on change to trigger DAO. Followed RFC 6550 6.3.1. No new comments added. 2 files changed.
